other_observers: nancy gobris (bander) mark peterson, brandon percival, many others
description: 2nd year male based on Pyle.  Sang a Mourning Warbler song (but not the galloping song heard in TV commercials) and gave a loud MOWA one-note call. Bird was well-studied in the hand.  The few white eye feathers were a concern with the slight possibility of a MOWA x MGWA.  Pyle does not show any measurements for undertail coverts as of value for separating MOWA vs. MGWA.  Greenish backed with gray hood and dark eye with a few white feathers below both the left eye and the bird's right eye. Dark bib.  Black lore, pink legs and feet.  Based on call note and song I would call it a Mourning Warbler.  When evaluating the entire population of both species hybrids would be very rare in nature.  Mourning Warbler is already rare enough in CO.
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
